articles.title: 7 leadership moves that matter before you step in front of your team

Tomorrow is the quarterly staff meeting, and project director Ann knows she needs to be ready. The agenda is familiar. At 3 p.m. she opens PowerPoint, pulls up the last deck, swaps in tomorrow’s date, and starts updating the numbers.



By 5:30 pm, the slides are done. But is she ready? She has a deck, not a message. She has data, not direction.

articles.summary: The Swedish cenral bank is urging consumers to horde a week's worth of cash in case of disruption to payment services during the volatile climate

articles.title: Father claims Google's AI product fuelled son's delusional spiral
articles.summary: The case is the first wrongful death case against Google over alleged harms caused by Gemini.

articles.title: X taps William Shatner to give out invites to its payments service, X Money
articles.summary: 42 X users donated to William Shatner's charity in exchange for an early invite to X Money beta.

articles.title: Regulator contacts Meta over workers watching intimate AI glasses videos
articles.summary: Videos, including of glasses-wearers using the toilet or having sex, are sometimes reviewed by a Kenya-based subcontractor.

articles.title: Remote work, offices shut: Tech giants scramble to respond as Iran war escalates
articles.summary: The region has been positioning itself as an AI hub, following billions of dollars in investment by tech giants.

articles.title: Video game Highguard axed weeks after release
articles.summary: It comes after the free-to-play game was given top billing at The Game Awards in December.

articles.title: Can an AI recruiter really spot a good carer?
articles.summary: AI is being used to screen care workers – but can a robot truly judge who is right for the job?

articles.title: Celebrating Holi
articles.summary: The Hindu festival, which celebrates the victory of good over evil, also marks the end of winter.

articles.title: TikTok won't protect DMs with controversial privacy tech, saying it would put users at risk
articles.summary: TikTok tells the BBC it won't join rival platforms such as WhatsApp and Messenger in using end-to-end encryption.

articles.title: Indian startup funding jumps 110% to $1.4b in February
articles.summary: Indian startup funding jumps 110% to $1.4b in February  Tech in Asia

articles.title: OpenAI changes deal with US military after backlash
articles.summary: Chief Executive Sam Altman said the group would prohibit the use of its systems to spy on Americans.
